author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
(prabhu he!)
 śuna mora duḥkhera kāhinī
viṣaya⋅halāhala, sudhābhāṇe piyalu̐,
āb avasāna dina-maṇi

khelārase śaiśava, paḍhoite kaiśora,
goyāolu̐, nā bhela viveka
bhoga-vaśe jauvane, ghara pāti’ basilu̐,
suta⋅mita bāḍala aneka

vṛddha-kāla āola, saba sukha bhāgala,
pīḍāvaśe hoinu kātara
sarvendriya durbala, kṣīṇa kalevara,
bhogā-bhāve duḥkhita antara

jñāna⋅laba⋅hīna, bhakti-rase vañcita,
āra mora ki ha’be upāya
patita bandhu tuhu̐, patitādhama hāma,
kṛpāya uṭhāo tava pā⋅ya

vicārite āvahi, guṇa nāhi pāobi,
kṛpā kara, choḍata vicāra
tava pada-paṅkaja⋅, sīdhu pibāota,
bhakativinode kara’ pāra
translation_intro: From The Harmonist, October 1927. Edited by Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati.
en_translation:
Hear, O Lord, the tale of my sorrows-- The poison of worldly enjoyment I have sipped, pretending it was the nectar; But now the sun is setting.

In the diversions of play my childhood, My boyhood in studies, I wasted; There arose no consciousness of good in me. For enjoying my youth, I devised a home and settled down in it; Children and friends did fast multiply.

Old age soon arrived, All joys departed; With disease I was smitten down. The senses lost their vigour, The body grew feeble, The heart was sad lacking enjoyment.

Devoid of a particle of true knowledge, Missing the elixir of devotion, What help is there for me? Thou art the Friend of the fallen; I am the worst of sinners; In mercy lift me to Thy feet.

Judging me Thou wilt find no good; Have pity, judge me not! The nectar of Thy lotus feet Make me taste; Do Thou deliver Bhaktivinode.
